text.title: Nginx Configuration Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the nginx config produced. Each pairs a binary directive check + Mozilla / RFC source.

rule.statement: `ssl_protocols` MUST list TLSv1.2 and TLSv1.3 only. TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 MUST NOT appear.

rationale.source: Mozilla SSL Configuration Generator, PCI DSS 3.2+

rule.rationale: TLS 1.0 / 1.1 are deprecated (PCI DSS 3.2+ disallows them) and contain known cryptographic weaknesses (BEAST, POODLE).

rule.statement: Every HTTPS server block MUST emit HSTS (`max-age` ≥ 6 months, `includeSubDomains`), X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY (or CSP frame-ancestors), and a Content-Security-Policy.
rule.rationale: These headers close clickjacking, MIME-sniffing, and downgrade attack surfaces. Modern browsers ignore the protections unless the headers are present.

rule.statement: Production server blocks MUST declare `http2` (or `http3` when available) on the listen directive. HTTP/1.1-only is acceptable only for legacy backends.
rule.rationale: HTTP/2 multiplexing reduces head-of-line blocking, cuts page load time 20-40 % on modern apps. HTTP/3 + QUIC adds resilience over lossy networks.

rule.statement: Auth endpoints (`/login`, `/oauth`, `/password-reset`) MUST sit inside a `limit_req` zone with rate ≤ 10 r/s per IP and burst ≤ 20.
rule.rationale: Without rate limiting, credential stuffing attacks slip past WAFs by staying under per-account thresholds. Per-IP throttling is the cheapest defence layer.

rule.statement: `gzip on;` MUST exclude already-compressed types (jpg, png, mp4, woff2). Use `gzip_types` whitelist; do NOT enable gzip globally on `*/*`.
rule.rationale: Re-compressing already-compressed media wastes CPU without saving bytes; double-compression can occasionally inflate size.

rule.statement: Every config change MUST be validated with `nginx -t` before `nginx -s reload`. CI hooks MUST run `nginx -t -c <new-config>` against a sandbox.
rule.rationale: A bad config that passes `reload` will fail on next restart, locking out the service when nobody is watching. `-t` catches it deterministically.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
